content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

December 6, 1988

Lilian S. Shek
Attorney at Law
Post Office Box 8148
Sacramento, CA 95818

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-88-424

Dear Ms. Shek:

This letter is in reply to your letter dated October 25, 1988,
which confirms our conversation concerning Renard F. Shepard's
filing requirements as a newly appointed judge. Judge Shepard
authorized you to contact us on his behalf.

Your summary of our telephone conversation with respect to the
filing deadline of Judge Shepard's assuming office statement of
economic interests (Form 721) and his requirement to complete only
Schedules A, B and C (investments and interests in real property) is
correct. The filing deadline is ten days after appointment.

I would like to clarify of the period covered for an annual
statement of economic interests when an official assumes office
between October 1 and December 31.

Regulation 18723(c) (copy enclosed) provides that if a person
assumes office between October 1 and December 31 and files an
assuming office statement, that person is not required to file an
annual statement until one year after the next annual filing
deadline. The annual filing deadline for judges is March 1.

Since Judge Shepard assumed office between October 1 and
December 31, 1988, his next filing obligation will be an annual
statement due no later than March 1, 1990. The period covered by
his annual statement will be the day after he assumed office through
December 31, 1989. (Regulation 18723 (a) and (b).)

If you have any questions regarding this advice, please call me
at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

[signature]

By: Linda Moureaux
Legal Documents Examiner

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Lilian S. Shek
Attorney at Law

Post Office Box 8148
Sacramento, CA 95818
(916) 455-5904

October 25, 1988

Ms. Linda Moureaux
California Fair Political Practices Commission
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
P.O. Box 207
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Ms. Moureaux:

Re: Filing of Statement of Economic Interests (Form 121)
     by Renard F. Shepard, Judicial Appointee to the
     Sacramento Municipal Court

This letter is to confirm our telephone conversation today,
wherein you advised me as to the following matters:

1. Based on the fact that Renard F. Shepard's judicial
appointment to the Sacramento Municipal Court is not
subject to the confirmation of the State Senate, the time within
which to file the Statement of Economic Interests (Form 121) is
ten (10) days after the close of the next ten days after the
date of appointment.

2. As judicial appointee assuming office on or about November
   1, 1988, the required to complete and file Schedules A, B, C, and D, but
   is not required through H.

3. Since Mr. Shepard's appointment falls within the period
   in question of October through December, the filing deadline for the
   initial statement to be barred is June 30, 1989. He must also file the
   annual statement the period from the filing date of the appointment
   through the statement to that of the next annual statement.

Please advise if there are any issues or questions regarding
our presentation. Thank you again for your assistance.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

Lilian S. Shek

Enclosures

cc: Renard F. Shepard

Lillian S. Shek
Attorney at Law
Post Office Box 8148
Sacramento, CA 95818
(916) 455-5904

October 25, 1988

Ms. Linda Moureaux
California Fair Political Practices Commission
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Ms. Moureaux:

Re: Filing of Statement of Economic Interests (Form 721)
by Renard F. Shepard, judicial appointee to the
Sacramento Municipal Court

This letter is to confirm our telephone conversation today,
wherein you advised me as to the following matters.

1. Based on the fact that Renard F. Shepard's judicial
appointment to the Sacramento Municipal Court bench is not
subject to the confirmation of the State Senate, the time within
which to file the Statement of Economic Interests (Form 721) is
ten (10) days after he takes office, and not ten days after the
date of his appointment.

2. As a judicial appointee assuming office for the first time,
he is required to complete and file Schedules A, B, and C, but
not Schedules D through H.

3. Since Mr. Shepard's appointment falls within the exception
period of October through December, the filing deadline for the
next annual statement will be March 1, 1990. The next statement
should cover the period from the filing date of the assuming
office statement to that of the next annual statement.

Please notify me immediately if I have misinterpreted any part of
your representation. Thank you again for your assistance.

Very truly yours,

Lillian S. Shek

LSS:sc
cc: Mr. Renard F. Shepard

Shek Attorney at Law Post Office Box 8148 Sacramento, CA 95818 Re: Your Request for Advice Our File No. A-88-424 Dear Ms. Shek: This letter is in reply to your letter dated October 25, 1988, which confirms our conversation concerning Renard F. Shepard's filing requirements as a newly appointed judge. Judge Shepard authorized you to contact us on his behalf. Your summary of our telephone conversation with respect to the filing deadline of Judge Shepard's assuming office statement of economic interests (Form 721) and his requirement to complete only Schedules A, B and C (investments and interests in real property) is correct. The filing deadline is ten days after appointment. I would like to clarify of the period covered for an annual statement of economic interests when an official assumes office between October 1 and December 31. Regulation 18723(c) (copy enclosed) provides that if a person assumes office between October 1 and December 31 and files an assuming office statement, that person is not required to file an annual statement until one year after the next annual filing deadline. The annual filing deadline for judges is March 1. Since Judge Shepard assumed office between October 1 and December 31, 1988, his next filing obligation will be an annual statement due no later than March 1, 1990. The period covered by his annual statement will be the day after he assumed office through December 31, 1989. (Regulation 18723 (a) and (b).) If you have any questions regarding this advice, please call me at (916) 322-5662. Sincerely, Diane M. Griffiths General Counsel By: Linda Moureaux Legal Documents Examiner This is a letter from Lilian S. Shek, an attorney at law, to Ms. Linda Moureaux of the California Fair Political Practices Commission. The letter discusses the filing requirements for the Statement of Economic Interests (Form 721) by Renard F. Shepard, who has been judicially appointed to the Sacramento Municipal Court. The key points in the letter are: 1. Based on the fact that Renard F. Shepard's judicial appointment to the Sacramento Municipal Court bench is not subject to confirmation by the State Senate, the time within which to file the Statement of Economic Interests (Form 721) is ten (10) days after he takes office, and not ten days after the date of his appointment. 2. As a judicial appointee assuming office for the first time, he is required to complete and file Schedules A, B, and C, but not Schedules D through H. 3. Since Mr. Shepard's appointment falls within the exemption period of October through December, the filing deadline for the next annual statement will be March 1, 1990. The next statement should cover the period from the filing date of the assuming office statement to that of the next annual statement. The letter concludes with a request for Ms. Moureaux to notify Mr. Shepard immediately if she is satisfied with the information provided. October 25, 1988 Ms. Linda Moureaux California Fair Political Practices Commission Technical Assistance
